carbon_policy_signal.item: EU-UK emissions trading agreement talks delayed by UK political crisis; rescheduling date unknown. EU emissions rose 2025, stalling decarbonisation; stricter Phase 4 auction schedules or MSR drawdowns likely.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Stalled decarbonisation forces tighter EU cap mechanics; MSR drawdowns and auction compression will support EUA futures. Linkage delay widens UKA-EUA arb window and defers regulatory certainty on cross-border trading, affecting power generation carbon-cost assumptions.

freshness_caveat: Coal (179 days old, as of 2025-12-26) and Clean Dark (174 days old, as of 2025-12-31) are stale; Clean Spark and DE Power current as of 2026-06-23. Coal and Clean Dark percentile ranks not used in forward-looking analysis; emphasis placed on Clean Spark, DE Power, TTF, Storage, and Renewables (all current or ≤2 days old).
risk_flags: Storage at 46.7% (22nd-pctile, 14.4 pp below seasonal); summer injection pace will dictate H1 2027 winter headroom and forward curve shape.
Clean Spark 57.89 EUR/MWh (96th-pctile) — highest coal (7th-pctile, 96 USD/t) in 5 years suggests structural gas-in-the-money regime persists; coal plant mothballing risk.
Asian LNG demand rising; Europe faces bidding competition on spot/forward arbitrage; TTF premium to LNG import parity vulnerable to 5–10% upside if Asian bids harden.
EU-UK ETS linkage talks postponed; regulatory clarity gap widens UKA-EUA arbitrage window; rescheduling date unknown — watch for European Council agenda closure end June.

base.summary: Storage refill steady; TTF holds 40–45 range; Clean Spark remains 50–60 EUR/MWh; gas-in-money regime persists.

downside.summary: Warm summer, EU demand softens; Asian LNG bids ease; storage injection accelerates; TTF front normalises to 35–40 range.

upside.summary: Asian LNG bids surge; European spot tightens; cold front or data-centre demand surprise; storage refill lags seasonal norm.

themes: Fuel-switch regime firmly in-the-money; gas-fired generation economically dominant
EU storage deficit extending into summer; refill pace critical for H2 adequacy
Asia-Europe LNG arbitrage widening; TTF upside risk on supply competition
Rising emissions + stalled decarbonisation; carbon policy drift amid EU-UK ETS linkage delay
top_drivers: Clean Spark at 96th-pctile and coal at 7th-pctile: gas-in-the-money fuel-switch regime is structural, not cyclical; coal plant retirement accelerating.
Storage 14.4 pp below seasonal at 46.7%; summer injection pace is the tightest H2 adequacy lever — any weather/demand shock compounds winter risk premium.
Asia-Europe LNG bidding competition intensifying; European TTF premium to LNG import parity under compression; upside to 5–10% if Asian bids or winter demand surprises.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 96th-percentile amid storage 14.4 pp below seasonal; Asian LNG competition tightens European gas supply while emissions rise, signalling structural gas demand headwinds.

watchlist.why: At 22nd-pctile and 14.4 pp below seasonal, injection trajectory will dictate winter 2026–27 adequacy and forward curve risk premium through Q4 2026.

watchlist.metric: TTF vs. LNG import parity spread
watchlist.why: Asian demand competition narrowing European access; any Asian bidding surge or European demand shock (data centres, AI baseload lobbying) will widen TTF premium and trigger fuel-switch acceleration.

watchlist.metric: EU-UK ETS linkage summit date & agenda
watchlist.why: Postponement removes near-term regulatory clarity; rescheduling signals will reshape UKA-EUA arb window and cross-border trading mechanics; material for June/July curve positioning.

watchlist.metric: Q3 2026 EU ETS auction calendar & MSR intake
watchlist.why: Rising 2025 emissions + stalled decarbonisation may trigger stricter Phase 4 auction schedules or MSR drawdowns; upward EUA bias if cap tightens ahead of summer demand.

watchlist.metric: Data centre baseload demand signals (July onwards)
watchlist.why: EU AI sector lobbying for gas-fired capacity; structural uplift to Cal+1 / Q+1 gas forwards if regulation permits or if renewable grids seen as insufficient.

watchlist_dated.name: European Council energy/trade agenda closure
watchlist_dated.why: May rescheduled EU-UK ETS linkage summit date; confirms regulatory clarity timeline for carbon arbitrage and cross-border trading mechanics.
